description_long: This is a tall, narrow-necked ceramic vase with a single curved handle extending from the neck to the shoulder. The body is decorated with classical red-figure motifs, including human figures and ornamental patterns, set against a black background. The front features a detailed depiction of a standing figure, while the sides and back display additional figures and stylized floral elements. The surface has a glossy finish, typical of ancient Greek pottery, and the handle is robust and smoothly integrated into the form. The base is flat, allowing the vase to stand securely on a pedestal or table. The overall craftsmanship and iconography suggest it is inspired by or replicates an ancient Greek lekythos or similar vessel.
description: A tall, black-glazed ceramic vase with a single handle and red-figure classical motifs, including human figures and floral patterns. The surface is glossy and finely detailed, evoking ancient Greek pottery.
description_view_0: The front view shows a prominent human figure and floral motifs, with the handle visible on the right side.
description_view_1: The rear view displays the plain black surface of the vase, with the handle fully visible and no decorative motifs.
description_view_2: This side view highlights another human figure in motion, with the handle on the left and additional ornamental details.
description_view_3: The opposite side view presents two figures facing each other, with decorative patterns between them and the handle not visible.
attribute_pattern: classical red-figure motifs with human and floral elements
